user_query:
I'd like a small event-triggered automation for my pilot portfolio updates. Treat the presence of input/drop/current_status.csv (compared to input/baseline_status.csv) as the event. When the script is run, it should detect and summarize changes and produce two artifacts:

1) output/status_summary.md – a concise, deterministic report (no runtime timestamps or machine-specific paths) using project_id as the unique key and only these tracked fields for change detection: status, milestone_date (schedule), risk_level. The report must contain the following sections with these exact section titles and content rules:
- Status changes: bullet one line per project where status changed, including project_id, project_name, owner, and "old_status -> new_status".
- Schedule changes: bullet one line per project where milestone_date changed, including project_id, project_name, owner, and "old_date -> new_date (delta_days=X)", where X = (new_date - old_date) in days, positive if pushed out, negative if pulled in.
- Risk changes: bullet one line per project where risk_level changed, including project_id, project_name, and "old_risk -> new_risk".
- New projects: bullet one line per project present only in current_status.csv, including project_id, project_name, owner, and status from current.
- Removed projects: bullet one line per project present only in baseline_status.csv, including project_id, project_name, owner, and status from baseline.

2) output/email_draft.txt – a plain-text email draft addressed using input/stakeholders.yaml. It must start with three header lines:
- To: a comma-separated list of recipients from stakeholders.yaml "to"
- Cc: a comma-separated list from stakeholders.yaml "cc" (leave empty if none)
- Subject: must begin with the stakeholders.yaml subject_prefix and include counts in parentheses exactly in this pattern: "<subject_prefix> (<projects_changed> changes, <high_risk> high-risk)" where:
  • projects_changed = count of unique project_ids where any of the tracked fields (status, milestone_date, risk_level) changed from baseline to current, excluding purely new/removed items
  • high_risk = count of projects in current_status.csv with risk_level == "High"

Body requirements:
- Greeting line: "Hi <greeting_name>," using stakeholders.yaml greeting_name
- A short paragraph summarizing counts for: projects_changed, new projects, removed projects, and high_risk (use the definitions above), referencing the new drop at input/drop/current_status.csv.
- Brief bulleted subsections that mirror the five sections from output/status_summary.md with at least one line per item in each category (if a category has no items, state "None").

Implementation notes:
- Compose content deterministically from the input files; do not include execution timestamps or environment-specific details.
- You may use any local scripting language; provide a runnable script (e.g., scripts/portfolio_update.py or scripts/portfolio_update.sh) that, when executed from the workspace root, reads the inputs and writes both outputs to the specified paths.

Inputs are provided under input/. Please generate the two outputs exactly at output/status_summary.md and output/email_draft.txt. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
